zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hatton於1200年出生在英國爾哈頓時，他的父親Hugh de Hatton26歲，母親Nichola Boydell20歲。他於大約1228年與Agnes de Dutton結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1280年在他的家鄉享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hatton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

variants.name: Hatten
meanings: English: variant of   Hatton  . This surname is also common among African Americans.
North German: habitational name from the name of an area of marshland between Oldenburg and Bremen.

variants.name: Hattan
meanings: English and Scottish (Argyll): variant of   Hatton  .

variants.name: Aton
meanings: variant of Hatton , with loss of initial H-.
topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)).

variants.name: Heath
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) heath’ (Middle English he(a)th, Old English hǣth) or else a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. The same word also denoted heather, the characteristic plant of heathland areas. This surname has also been established in Dublin, Ireland, since the late 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When William Hatton was born in 1200, in Hatton, Cheshire, England, his father, Hugh de Hatton, was 26 and his mother, Nichola Boydell, was 20. He married Agnes de Dutton about 1228. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1280, in his hometown, at the age of 80.

Il a épousé Agnes de Dutton environ 1228. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1280, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 80 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Hatton(이)가 1200년 영국 체실르 해턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de Hatton(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Nichola Boydell(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1228년 Agnes de Dutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1280년 그의 고향에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.
